type_explanation: Per-task scores predicted from review of underlying prompt + standard at this commit. NOT from a real Claude session execution. Real execution baseline requires fresh Claude sessions running each eval input and human/automated rubric scoring. This paper baseline establishes a starting line for regression detection — when a future real execution drops a task >5% below paper baseline, investigate.

eval-001.notes: Mature prompt with §4.0 placeholder resolution + §4.5 auto-capture (from 2026-04-30 commits). Field-tested on morning-digest. Risk: residual ~/cortex-x/ source-internal refs (D-4 in MIGRATIONS.md) still present in 17 files — could leak into scaffolded output. Path fix in c66bd2d covered template + agents but not all source files.

eval-002.notes: BAIL flow exists in new-project.md but is less heavily field-tested than full flow. Real risk of Claude 'being helpful' and running Phase 2 research despite skip signal. eval-002 IS the canary for this drift; expect this to be the lowest score. If real run scores ≥0.65, BAIL discipline is intact.

eval-003.notes: Slim 5-section schema documented explicitly. Anti-Tech-Stack rule in prompt. Risk: LLM 'helpfulness' adding Tech Stack/Directory sections to be thorough. Schema restraint is hardest to guarantee — score reflects this tension.

eval-004.notes: 4-field format explicit, anti-patterns enumerated, append-only contract clear. Strong prompt. Risk: emotional context creep (one of the listed anti-patterns) in transcript-heavy sessions.

eval-005.notes: ssot-enforcer is mandatory per code-review.md. Rule 1 = BLOCKER explicit in standards/RULE-1.md. Should catch a planted 3-file duplicate. Risk: partial finding (cites 2 of 3 files), or assigning WARN instead of BLOCK.

eval-006.notes: security-auditor was extended in 9323c25 with 8-layer + Agentic Security § + Browser Automation §. Should catch SQL injection (Layer 4). RLS bypass detection (Layer 3 Authz secondary finding) is the harder-to-guarantee piece. If real run catches injection but misses service-role-key issue, score lands at 0.8.

eval-007.notes: Doctor §1 explicitly checks hook registration in settings.json. Pattern is deterministic (file-existence + key-presence). Risk: copy-paste-ready fix snippet has wrong syntax (e.g., trailing comma) — minor.

eval-008.notes: Parser logic lives in session-start.cjs (deterministic JS, not LLM judgment). Output prompt is well-structured. Highest-confidence eval — score should track closely between paper and real run.

eval-009.notes: 4-question format + [TRANSFERABLE] tagging explicit. Risk: in long sessions LLM produces 6+ entries (fatigue territory) or skips Q4. Mock-vs-real test DB lesson — LLM might classify as project-specific instead of transferable, missing the cortex-x value.

eval-010.notes: Hard gates explicit in cortex-evolve.md §B.2 with thresholds in config/evolve.yaml. Anti-hallucination is explicit, prompt forbids in-flight threshold lowering. Highest-stakes eval (proves framework honesty) and likely highest pass rate due to explicit numerical gates.

weakest_task.reason: BAIL flow respect-user-signal discipline is the least field-tested. Worth running manually first.

next_steps: Manual run priority order: eval-002 (weakest) → eval-010 (highest stakes) → eval-005 + eval-006 (review pipeline canaries) → others
Real execution requires fresh Claude session per eval. Until automated runner exists, run 1-2 evals per week.
After 3+ real runs, retire this paper-baseline; switch to real-baseline for delta tracking.
If any task scores >0.10 below paper baseline on real run, investigate prompt drift or rubric calibration.
advisory_status: ADVISORY ONLY — do NOT block PR merges on this baseline. Real-execution baseline must establish before gating.
